cards.title: Tattoos in Japan
cards.body: In Japan, tattoos have a long history and are associated with the Yakuza, but they were originally used to mark social status and spiritual beliefs.

cards.title: Day of the Dead
cards.body: Mexico's Day of the Dead is a celebration to honor loved ones who have passed away, with altars, decorations, and traditional foods like pan de muerto.

cards.title: Kintsugi Art
cards.body: Kintsugi is a Japanese art form that repairs broken pottery with gold or silver, highlighting the brokenness rather than disguising it.

cards.title: Henna Traditions
cards.body: In many cultures, henna is used to stain skin and hair, and is often associated with special occasions like weddings and festivals.

cards.title: Geisha Culture
cards.body: Geishas are traditional Japanese entertainers who are trained in arts like music, dance, and poetry, and are known for their distinctive clothing and makeup.

cards.title: Carnival Celebrations
cards.body: Carnival is a festive season that occurs before Lent, and is celebrated with parades, music, and costumes in many countries around the world.

cards.title: Maori Face Tattoos
cards.body: Traditional Maori face tattoos, called Ta Moko, are intricate designs that signify status, rank, and spirituality in Maori culture.

cards.title: Sikh Turbans
cards.body: The Sikh turban, called a Dastar, is a symbol of spirituality and identity, and is worn by Sikh men as a reminder of their faith and values.

cards.title: Navajo Weaving
cards.body: Navajo weaving is a traditional art form that involves creating intricate patterns and designs on looms, often using wool and other natural materials.

cards.title: Bollywood Dance
cards.body: Bollywood dance is a fusion of traditional and modern styles, often featuring intricate footwork and energetic movements, and is a popular form of entertainment in India.
